behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is between request-builder and API client wiring. A unit test of only the builder can pass while integration still fails if serialization/adapter drops the labels field. Integration-brake: no real OS/network condition is required (stub transport is enough), no dependence on model output content, no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about request payload completeness, not live provider behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that captures outbound payload; invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert captured request contains the full taxonomy list exactly once and untruncated.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure can occur at module handoff from response parser to normalization (e.g., parser path bypasses validator). A pure unit test of normalization can pass while end-to-end module call still leaks unexpected labels. Integration-brake: no real OS/network needed, no model-quality dependence, no multi-step agentic sequence. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text specifies handling of an explicit returned label, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with stubbed API response returning an out-of-taxonomy label; assert public classify() returns "unknown" and never returns raw invalid value.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization logic on identical input, within one local function/object boundary and no I/O. No cross-module interaction is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants already fixed in fixture) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI decisions. Adjacent-counterexample (System lower level): a real-HTTP test against a deterministic stub endpoint always returns same label and passes, while real provider model can vary across calls and fail consistency. LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on actual model outputs over repeated calls. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior requires repeated single-step classification calls, not multiple agentic stages with inter-step handoff logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live calls (same doc/taxonomy, fixed model params where possible), persist raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when corresponding provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often at handoff between budget-check logic and dispatch path (e.g., over-budget request still sent). A unit test for token policy alone can pass while module still dispatches incorrectly. Integration-brake: can be reproduced with in-process stubs; no real OS/network/subprocess required; no dependence on model output quality; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic/System because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement logic.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget, call classify() with oversized input, and use stub transport to assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) is applied before send; verify no over-budget payload is dispatched.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real AI provider accounting/tokenization behavior. Adjacent-counterexample (System lower level): a real HTTP test against a non-model stub can report fixed low usage and pass, while real model/provider token accounting exceeds budget for the same input. LLM-DEP default applies because execution path requires real AI/ML API behavior; mocks hide this failure. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because only a single model call’s usage is evaluated, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification calls for representative documents, capture provider-reported usage fields, and assert each call’s reported tokens <= configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry/backoff orchestration between API client, timer/backoff policy, and caller-facing result. A unit test of retry helper alone can pass while module wiring still returns error immediately. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required if stub can emit rate-limit response and then success; no model-output dependence; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about handling a specific provider signal and retry policy, which is reproducible with in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API that returns rate-limit error then success; run classify() with test clock/backoff hooks and assert it waits/retries and eventually returns success instead of propagating first error.
